levels.label: Dreamer
levels.copy: The Founder Readiness Radar pattern is still mostly informal or ad hoc. Pick one stabilising action, document it, and avoid adding a long process before the basics are real.

levels.label: Starter
levels.copy: Some Founder Readiness Radar practices exist, but they still depend on individual memory and effort. The next step is two repeatable routines with clear owners.

levels.label: Builder
levels.copy: Core Founder Readiness Radar practices are repeatable, but evidence quality and ownership are uneven. Work on the weakest dimension before optimising the strongest one.

levels.label: Operator
levels.copy: Founder Readiness Radar practices are measured and connected to decisions. The main work is now removing the constraint that prevents the system from scaling cleanly.

levels.label: Venture-Ready
levels.copy: Founder Readiness Radar is operating as a learning system. Keep calibrating against evidence, stress-test resilience, and avoid treating a strong score as a permanent guarantee.

recommendations.opportunity-insight: Run 15 problem interviews in one narrow segment and document repeated pains, triggers, and current alternatives.
recommendations.execution-discipline: Introduce a weekly outcome, one hypothesis-driven experiment, and a decision log.
recommendations.resourcefulness-and-economics: Create a 13-week cash forecast and a map of people, partners, channels, and non-cash resources.
recommendations.resilience-and-sustainability: Define personal risk signals, protected recovery time, and one task to delegate or stop this month.

defaultSecondAction: Retake the diagnostic after a focused 30-day improvement cycle and compare only your own progress unless a team comparison is explicitly agreed.
reflectionQuestion: Which assumption behind your weakest dimension deserves the fastest evidence check?
methodologyNote: The current version uses four dimensions with two questions each. Dimension scores are normalised to 0-100. The balanced index weights 70% average dimension performance and 30% the weakest dimension. No percentile or benchmark is shown until a compatible sample exists.
shareNote: Share cards reveal only the level and strongest area. They do not publish individual answers, sensitive weaknesses, legal or financial details, security gaps, or family-specific information.
